data.narrative:
Xage Security is a Series B-stage defense and security company that has raised $80M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable set spanning 10 private and 2 public firms operating in the Defense & Security sector, with primary focus on 6 same-stage private peers including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression methodology, using total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yields $152M. Peer-median analysis across the 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a $1.2B implied valuation, with the peer set ranging from $1.0B (Castelion, Defense Unicorns) to $1.5B (Vannevar Labs, Dream Security).

The two methods produce a wide range reflecting material dispersion in the comparable set. A triangulated median across both methods suggests $676M. The full analytical range spans $129M (low) to $1.4B (high). The stage-progression result anchors conservatively; peer-median dominates the upper bound. Key uncertainties include relative product maturity and revenue traction versus named peers, and whether Xage's specific technology positioning and customer concentration justify peer valuations at the $1.0B–$1.5B band or warrant a discounted entry point closer to the $200M–$400M midpoint.
